approval_status: Confirmatory owner approval #14 is not granted. This PR may freeze the template hash but cannot authorize confirmatory execution.
seal_method: The pilot PR merge freezes this confirmatory template against outcome-driven adaptation but is not its execution seal. Only after a passing pilot result may a separate confirmatory-only PR record approval #14, change kind to pre_execution_sealed_plan, set approval_granted_by_this_file and execution_authorized_by_this_file to true, and merge; that later merge commit is the confirmatory execution seal.
question: After an independent feasibility pilot passes, do a sales-only elderly-understanding clarification and a circulated provisional-chat-approval contradiction change their corresponding R1/R2 and R4 structural loss-event occurrence rates in fresh same-seed S2 pairs?

measurement_boundary.primary: Opportunity-level structural loss-event occurrence for the endpoint assigned to each contrast.
measurement_boundary.direct_detection: Current R1-R4 catalog is uncovered, so direct miss count/rate/interval are N/A; uncovered event counts and related signals are reported separately.
measurement_boundary.r1_r2: Structural candidate only; semantic adequacy of customer-contact records is not judged.
measurement_boundary.readiness: This experiment and its report do not update acceptance or readiness.

wave_execution_policy.pair_content: Each wave contains one complete R1 control/treatment pair and one complete R4 control/treatment pair.
wave_execution_policy.credit_check: Apply the batch-sealed fail-closed seven-credit guard before every wave launch.
wave_execution_policy.continue_basis: Proceed only after the prior wave and its infrastructure retries complete and required integrity artifacts validate.
wave_execution_policy.integrity_checkpoint_enforcement: Pre-spend order, duplicate-launch, commit, plan, batch, credit, and retry controls are machine-enforced. Post-run loss/monitoring/manipulation integrity before the next wave remains procedural in this Draft template; before confirmatory execution approval, add a machine-validated integrity receipt or explicitly approve the manual checkpoint as a residual risk.
wave_execution_policy.outcome_peeking: Effect direction, arm rates, paired deltas, and significance must not be computed or used between waves.
wave_execution_policy.stopping_rule: Stop for unavailable/insufficient credits, unresolved infrastructure failure, or integrity failure; never stop or continue because of an observed treatment effect.

contrasts.hypothesis: The clarification reduces R1/R2 structural candidate occurrence; treatment-minus-control is expected to be non-positive.
The contradiction increases R4 unapproved-completion occurrence; treatment-minus-control is expected to be non-negative.

preregistered_measurements.method: Opportunity-level event rate for P-01/P-10, arm Wilson 95%, all five same-seed treatment-minus-control deltas, and their mean. Eligible-run incidence is secondary.
preregistered_measurements.decision_rule: Directional candidate only if the mean paired delta is negative and at least 4/5 non-null seed deltas are negative. This 4/5 sign rule is a screening heuristic, not a 5% significance test. Zero, mixed, or opposite results mean no directional candidate and remain inconclusive about equivalence/no effect; they are reported without reseeding. This first loss-outcome campaign cannot by itself create a confirmed finding; confirmation requires a separately sealed fresh-seed run.

preregistered_measurements.method: Opportunity-level event rate for P-04/P-11, arm Wilson 95%, all five same-seed treatment-minus-control deltas, and their mean. Eligible-run incidence is secondary.
preregistered_measurements.decision_rule: Directional candidate only if the mean paired delta is positive and at least 4/5 non-null seed deltas are positive. This 4/5 sign rule is a screening heuristic, not a 5% significance test. Zero, mixed, or opposite results mean no directional candidate and remain inconclusive about equivalence/no effect; they are reported without reseeding. This first loss-outcome campaign cannot by itself create a confirmed finding; confirmation requires a separately sealed fresh-seed run.

preregistered_measurements.method: Report catalog coverage, uncovered loss-event counts, direct miss metrics, right censoring, and related-signal presence exactly as loss_event_campaign.v1 emits them.
preregistered_measurements.decision_rule: With the sealed current catalog, direct miss metrics must be N/A for uncovered endpoints. Related R4 notices never count as direct capture and no detection-rate finding is promoted.

preregistered_measurements.method: Global R3 unverified-completion sentinel over all completions, with contrast-arm exercise counts shown separately.
preregistered_measurements.decision_rule: Any R3 event or fewer than one campaign-total R3 opportunity makes causal_interpretation_allowed=false and prevents every primary delta from promotion. If the global gate passes but a contrast arm has zero R3 opportunities, primary occurrence screening may still be applied, but that arm cannot support an arm-specific R3 integrity claim. Every contrast-arm exercise gap is reported.

preregistered_measurements.method: List every loss event outside the endpoint assigned to that run's contrast.
preregistered_measurements.decision_rule: Report descriptively as possible spillover; do not silently pool into the assigned primary endpoint and do not invalidate an otherwise valid primary comparison.

secondary_g3_contract.role: Secondary intermediate-evidence analysis only; it does not enter any M3 loss-event decision rule.
secondary_g3_contract.execution: If G3 is run, fix these values, rerun triage afterward, and report its cost/evidence separately. M3 loss-event aggregation does not wait for G3.

execution_conditions.seeds_note: No seed 940-949 appears in tracked experiment plans/specs or local run meta files as of 2026-07-10.
execution_conditions.no_reseeding_rule: Infrastructure failures retry the same run ID and seed after explicit partial-root deletion; outcome-based replacement is forbidden.
execution_conditions.retry_manifest_rule: For each wave, preserve its initial four-run manifest. Every retry must use a new --batch-dir, retain that wave_id, and contain exactly the prior failed run IDs. Supply the complete ordered wave/retry manifest chain to aggregation.

known_power_risk.evidence: Local ledgers for 20 recent related 40-tick S2 D1 confirmation runs and 12 D1b runs contain zero contract_completed/documents_delivered events (0/32 runs, verified 2026-07-10).
known_power_risk.implication: R1/R2 and R4 structural loss events, and the R3 sentinel opportunities, all require a completion. A separately sealed four-run pilot now tests that floor before any confirmatory run, and the confirmatory campaign is split into five fixed waves. The pilot reduces avoidable spend but does not increase statistical power or remove the risk of sparse confirmatory outcomes. Conditionally, if the R1/R2 control arm remains at zero occurrence, treatment-minus-control cannot be negative and the preregistered benefit direction cannot be supported; R4 harm remains mathematically possible but shares the same completion floor.
known_power_risk.decision_required: Do not seal or run this confirmatory plan unless the independent pilot report passes its preregistered feasibility gate. A passing pilot authorizes only consideration of this separately sealed K=5 plan; pilot effects are forbidden and cannot justify changing the confirmatory design.

cost_guard.rule: The sealed batch guard is authoritative and is reapplied before each four-run wave. Abort before launching a wave when available balance cannot be read or is below 7 credits. This is a launch floor, not a total campaign budget or spending cap.

proposed_execution_order: Review this Draft as the pilot execution PR: the pilot plan may be approved and sealed here, while this confirmatory artifact remains a hash-frozen, non-executable template. Run the pilot at the exact merge commit and publish a result proving pilot_gate.passed=true; a failed or missing pilot result is a hard no-go.
After the passing pilot result exists, approve and merge this confirmatory plan separately; its later merge commit becomes the confirmatory seal. Pilot artifacts and seeds remain excluded.

Check out that exact confirmatory merge commit with a clean working tree, confirm the imported company_twin module resolves to this checkout, and use the same commit for all five waves, retries, per-run artifacts, and aggregation.

Run wave-1 through wave-5 in the sealed order. Invoke run-batch with --batch-spec, --plan, and --wave plus a distinct batch directory for each wave; exact clean-HEAD bytes, authorization metadata, persistent wave state, and the seven-credit fail-closed guard are checked before every launch.

After each wave, retry only manifest-recorded infrastructure failures with identical run IDs/seeds and a distinct retry batch directory. Validate required per-run artifacts and integrity before the next wave, but do not compute or inspect effect direction, arm rates, paired deltas, or significance.
For every completed run, write loss_events.json and loss_event_monitoring.json.

After every sealed wave completes, run loss-event-campaign with all initial wave manifests and retries in exact wave order; it automatically evaluates the sealed manipulation gate for every control and treatment run.
If the manipulation gate fails, retain the diagnostic report and stop with exit 1; pair exclusion, seed replacement, post-hoc successful-run rerun, and causal interpretation are forbidden.
Review integrity gates and unexpected-loss rows before interpreting paired occurrence deltas.
Publish results and artifact hashes in a separate result PR; do not update readiness automatically.
owner_decisions_required_before_confirma: Approve the independent four-run feasibility pilot as a hard prerequisite, its separate merge seal, and permanent exclusion of pilot seeds/results from K=5.
Approve the two mutation-to-endpoint assignments and K=5 fresh confirmatory seed blocks.
Approve the fixed isolation conditions: S2, 40 ticks, measurement prompts, openrouter:qwen/qwen3.6-flash, circulation enabled, time pressure off, consequences off, and motives off.
Approve opportunity as the primary occurrence unit rather than eligible-run incidence.
Approve pre-event signals as warning-only and a two-tick (one business day) post-event window.
Approve R3 minimum=1 at campaign-total scope rather than requiring every contrast arm to exercise R3.
Approve plan-external loss events as descriptive rather than an integrity failure.
Approve running M3 occurrence-first with direct miss rates N/A, without adding a new agent-visible discovery control.

Approve exact tick-1 full-text mutation delivery to every active seat whose role is listed in the mutation visible_roles, before the first assigned endpoint opportunity; controls must have no document circulation.
Approve the 4/5 directional candidate screening rules and secondary-only G3 contract.
Approve the fixed five-wave order, four runs per wave, concurrency two, seven-credit fail-closed check before every wave, and the ban on outcome-based early stopping or continuation.

Before confirmatory execution approval, choose and seal either a machine-validated per-wave integrity receipt or an explicitly owned manual integrity checkpoint; runner subprocess success alone is not evidence that loss/monitoring/manipulation artifacts passed.
Accept that a passing feasibility pilot reduces avoidable spend but does not remove sparse-outcome or low-power risk in the confirmatory campaign.

Confirm that approval #14 remains withheld for this template; only after a passing pilot result may a separate confirmatory-only PR update approval_status, change kind to pre_execution_sealed_plan, and set both approval/execution booleans=true.
